edges.description: Disrupted distal limb morphogenesis shortens the first metacarpal.
Abnormal distal limb patterning affects thumb phalangeal development.
Abnormal distal limb patterning affects first-ray foot development.
Incomplete Mullerian duct fusion produces a bicornuate or didelphic uterus.
Disrupted urogenital developmental patterning contributes to hypospadias in affected males.

nodes.description: Partially divided (bicornuate) or completely divided (didelphic) uterus in affected females, representing defects of Mullerian duct fusion. Common in affected females.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Uterine anomalies are common in females with HFG, and typically involve a partially divided (bicornuate) or completely divided (didelphic) uterus, representing defects of Müllerian duct fusion

nodes.description: HOXA13 is a homeobox transcription factor that patterns distal limb structures. Loss-of-function mutations (nonsense, missense, or polyalanine expansions) disrupt DNA binding or protein function, leading to abnormal morphogenesis of distal metacarpals, phalanges, and metatarsals. Multiple mutation mechanisms act through different pathways, and mutations can produce more severe limb phenotypes than initially recognized.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: the identification of a HOXA13 nonsense mutation in a family with hand-foot-genital syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Novel HOXA13 mutations and the phenotypic spectrum of hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in HOXA13 can therefore cause more-severe limb abnormalities than previously suspected and may act by more than one mechanism

evidence.reference_title: Novel HOXA13 mutations and the phenotypic spectrum of hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: nonsense mutations truncating the encoded protein N-terminal to or within the homeodomain produce typical limb and genitourinary abnormalities

nodes.description: Ventrally displaced urethral opening in affected males, with variable severity.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: affected males may have hypospadias (ventrally misplaced urethral opening) of variable severity

nodes.description: HOXA13 is also required for Mullerian duct fusion during female reproductive tract development. Loss-of-function mutations lead to incomplete fusion, resulting in bicornuate or didelphic uterus in affected females. In males, the same developmental program underlies hypospadias.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: the identification of a HOXA13 nonsense mutation in a family with hand-foot-genital syndrome

nodes.description: Shortened first metacarpals of normal thickness, a hallmark hand anomaly distinguishing HFGS from other brachydactyly syndromes.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Limb anomalies include short first metacarpals of normal thickness, small distal phalanges of the thumbs, short middle phalanges of the fifth fingers

nodes.description: Shortened great toe due to a short first metatarsal and small, pointed distal phalanx. This is the characteristic foot anomaly.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: the great toe is shorter due to a short first metatarsal and a small, pointed distal phalanx

nodes.description: Small distal phalanges of the thumbs, contributing to overall thumb shortening.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of HOXA13 in hand-foot-genital syndrome.
evidence.snippet: small distal phalanges of the thumbs
